steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows a comma after "its language" and precedes "spoken by most current residents of Argentina." The comma introduces an appositive phrase that renames "its language." The blank must keep that phrase from becoming an independent clause (which would create a comma splice).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — being: "Spanish being spoken by most current residents" is a participial phrase — no subject-verb pair, no comma splice. ✓
  • Choice B — is: "Spanish is spoken" is a full independent clause joined to the first by only a comma — comma splice. ✗
  • Choice C — is being: Same problem as B — creates an independent clause after a comma. ✗
  • Choice D — will be: Also forms an independent clause and shifts to future tense, inconsistent with the present context. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
